sourceMovie.title: Labyrinth
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Labyrinth for the same reason it broke through in 1986: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a family rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Jim Henson keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The adventure and family blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: The Wizard of Oz
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Labyrinth clicked for you, The Wizard of Oz (1939) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and family, with earned warmth without mush. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Labyrinth clicked for you, The Lord of the Rings: The Fellowship of the Ring (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in adventure and fantasy, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Labyrinth?
faq.answer: Try The Wizard of Oz, The Spiderwick Chronicles, Inkheart—each shares DNA with Labyrinth while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Labyrinth?
faq.answer: Labyrinth (1986) is best described as Adventure, Family, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Labyrinth worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this adventure lane.
